x: Incorporates ISO 13485:2016 by reference, replacing decades-old QSR. Major harmonization for EU-based manufacturers.

t: South Korea: Fast-Track + AI Basic Act + DMPA
x: Three developments: market access cut to 80–140 days, healthcare AI classified High-Impact, pre-approved change management for algorithms.
Refreshed AI in Healthcare Guidelines with seven ethical principles including generative AI provisions.
Revised risk management standard integrating functional safety and cybersecurity into a unified framework. Effective 1 Mar 2027.

t: MHRA Indefinite CE Mark Recognition — Consultation Closed
x: Proposes permanent recognition of CE-marked devices in GB. ~90% of devices on the GB market are CE-marked. Result pending.

t: CNIL/HAS AI Healthcare Guide — Consultation Closes
x: Joint guide on AI deployment in healthcare covering acquisition through uninstallation, governance, and generative AI specifics.

t: HIPAA Security Rule Overhaul — Finalization Target
x: First major update since 2013. MFA, encryption, network segmentation mandatory. 24-hour incident reporting for business associates.
Proposal 2025/0404: Revised Rule 11 reclassifies most MDSW as Class I by default. Also proposes removing medical devices from AI Act high-risk scope.
Only v2.0-certified hosters may legally host health data for third parties. Reinforced EEA-only hosting, stronger ISO 27001 alignment.
Major revision: three safety classes replaced by two Software Process Accuracy Levels. Expanded scope to all health software. AI/ML process planning included.
Actor Registration, UDI/Device Registration, NB & Certificates, Market Surveillance. All economic operators must have an SRN.
Conformity assessment body notification deadline. MD under MDR/IVDR excluded, but peripheral apps and cloud services may be in scope.
UDI requirements for Class IIb and III devices including SaMD begin in Australia.
Original timeline for high-risk AI rules. Digital Omnibus may delay to Dec 2027 (Annex III) or Aug 2028 (Annex I/medical devices). MDR simplification could remove MD from scope entirely.
Two Software Process Accuracy Levels (I, II) replace Classes A/B/C. Scope expanded to all health software. AI/ML process planning. Aligned with IEC 82304 and IEC 81001.
Complete implementation of basic security measures required. Medical device manufacturers are in scope. Max fines EUR 10M / 2% turnover.
Actively exploited vulnerabilities must be reported to ENISA within 24 hours.

t: CEN-CENELEC JTC 21 — AI Act Harmonised Standards
x: Target for 10 standards: risk management, data governance, transparency, human oversight, cybersecurity, QMS, conformity assessment, post-market monitoring.

t: prEN 18286 — AI Act QMS Standard Revised
x: First harmonised standard for AI Act QMS (Article 17). Failed Jan 2026 vote with 1,288 comments. Under revision since March 2026.
Second Statutory Instrument: SaMD up-classified to at least Class IIa, mirroring EU MDR. Includes UDI, international reliance scheme.
All existing devices on the EU market must be registered in EUDAMED by this date.
Migration from Azure to SecNumCloud-certified sovereign cloud. Signals de facto standard for health data hosting in France.
Regulatory rulebook for AI/ML medical devices. Will shape the UK’s bespoke AI medical device framework.
UDI requirements extend to Class II medical devices in Brazil.
European Health Data Space implementing acts must be adopted. Framework for cross-border health data exchange.
Unified risk framework (functional safety + cybersecurity) for medical device software becomes mandatory in China.
Notified bodies must complete retrospective registration of all certificates.
Realistic earliest adoption of the simplification proposal including Rule 11 revision and AI Act scope changes.

t: AI Act High-Risk Annex III (If Delayed)
x: Potential delayed application date for high-risk AI systems in Annex III under the Digital Omnibus proposal.
All CRA requirements become fully applicable for products with digital elements.
UDI requirements extend to Class I devices, completing the full phase-in across all risk classes.
Revision under review since Jan 2025. Expected to include cybersecurity and AI provisions. Current version (2016) remains in force until publication.

t: AI Act Annex I / Medical Devices (If Delayed)
x: Potential delayed application for AI systems covered under EU harmonisation legislation including medical devices.

t: UK MHRA — MDD Transitional Period End
x: Devices under old EU MDD must transition. Extended to Dec 2028 under the CE recognition proposal.
Patient Summaries and ePrescriptions provisions become applicable across the EU.
All transitional periods expired. Full EUDAMED functionality. Harmonised standards for AI Act expected to be stable.
Mid-point of the successor WHO strategy currently under global consultation.
